personas.name: Daniel, Frustrated Subscriber
personas.description: Daniel is a 34 year old high school teacher from Toronto, Canada who signed up for Calm's free 7-day trial after seeing a social media ad. He is not very tech savvy and doesn't read the subscription fine prints. He expected to explore the app risk-free, but got a $79.99 charge on his bank account
goals: Trying out a meditation app before paying a full subscription
pain_points: Getting an unexpected fee/charge before the trial ends
context: Cancels the subsciption on the day the trial ends
constraints: Billing, fees and charges should be transparent
Requesting a refund should be fairly easy

personas.name: Ad-Overwhelmed, Emma
personas.description: Emma is a first year university student, who uses the free version of the calm app at nights to fall asleep easier. However, everytime she tries to open the app, she has to go through many popup ads to get to the actual app. Also everytime she's about to fall asleep an ad shows up which makes it harder for her to sleep
goals: Listen to calm audios at night to fall asleep
Use the free version without bombardment of ads
Access calming content immediately upon opening the app
pain_points: Having to X out of many ads when opening the app
Feeling pressured into purchasing the premium subscription
Getting an egregious amount of subscribe now ads
context: Uses the app during moments of stress or anxiety
Uses the app in quiet environments
constraints: Interruptions must be minimal during use
Audio Ads shouldn't play when calming content is playing

personas.name: Walter, Frustrated Senior User
personas.description: Walter is a 67 year old retired grandfather. He has very low knowledge of technoclogy. He uses calm for daily meditation practice. However, he becomes increasingly frustrated when bugs and performance issues disrupt his routine
goals: Use the calm app to practice meditation
pain_points: The app crashing out of nowhere
context: Uses the app during structured routines/times
Wakes up frequently at night and relies on the app to fall back asleep
constraints: No interruptions (technical difficulties or app crashing during meditation)

personas.name: Ignored customer, Batrice
personas.description: Beatrice is a 43 year old doctor. She is a paying customer who encounters issues and feels neglected due to very slow or even nonexistent responses from customer support, leading to frustration and loss of trust
goals: Resolve technical or billing issues quickly
Receive timely and helpful responses from customer support
pain_points: Getting an automated email with no actual help
Feeling ignored after multiple attempts of getting in contact with customer service
Having to talk to AI to solve an issue
constraints: Not tech-savvy so needs active help from customer support
Doesn't have time to follow-up many times to get a response

personas.name: Anxious, Alex
personas.description: Alex is a 29 year old psychology graduate student in Vancouver, Canada living with generalized anxiety disorder. He was introduced to Calm by his therapist two years ago
goals: Build a consistent daily meditation habit
Control panic attacks from his generalized anxiety disorder
pain_points: Disruptions (techincal bugs/glitches or app crashes) during meditation sessions or panic attacks
Needs content that matches emotional state
context: Uses the app to replaces habits like social media scrolling
Deals with anxiety and panic attacks
constraints: Content must to be accessible quickly during anxiety episodes
Listeting sessions should be uninterrupted and no sudden distruptions
